title: Splunk Scheduled Search Skipping, Concurrency Saturation, and Per-User Quota Exhaustion Detection
dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler for status=skipped and status=success lines with reason, savedsearch_id, app, user, dispatch_time, run_time, and result_count when present; index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component=DispatchManager OR component=SearchScheduler for dispatch and scheduler daemon narratives; index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage component=PerProcess with search_props.app for active_hist_searches style concurrency hints; index=_audit action=search for user-level search audit volume; lookup splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv for owner_team, business_priority, cron_schedule; optional index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_search_head_inventory for shc_label and env_tier context
description: Detects scheduled search dispatch skipping and starvation by fusing scheduler.log skipped and success cadence, splunkd DispatchManager and SearchScheduler pressure language, introspection PerProcess concurrency, and _audit search volume, then attributing outcomes per search_head, user, app, and savedsearch_id with inventory enrichment for cron thundering herds, concurrency ceilings, and quota exhaustion patterns.
value: Operations and product owners keep compliance dashboards, security correlations, and revenue reports on schedule: this control exposes when the platform silently drops scheduled work due to slot starvation or unfair quotas, quantifies blast radius per owner team, and preserves an evidence trail that distinguishes intentional disables from capacity incidents before executives notice missing slices.
grandmaExplanation: We run a busy kitchen with a fixed number of stoves. When too many tickets demand the same second, some orders get skipped—not because the recipe vanished, but because every burner was busy. We catch that early, separate a true capacity crunch from unfair line rules or a badly timed rush, and steer teams to the right fix before anyone goes hungry.
implementation: Schedule platform_uc_13_1_5_scheduled_search_skip_concurrency every fifteen minutes from a Monitoring Console or fleet search head with earliest=-4h@h, keep splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv and CMDB splunk_search_head_inventory current, tune skip_rate and burst thresholds per pool class, route critical rows to platform on-call with workload drilldowns, and archive weekly CSV exports to your governance index for operational reviews.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the search platform engineering team that operates search heads and search head clusters, the identity and access management stewards who approve role and quota changes, and the observability engineers who curate Monitoring Console views plus the configuration management owners who publish limits.conf and savedsearches.conf baselines. This use case isolates scheduled search dispatch starvation: skipped scheduler.log lines, concurrency ceiling language, per-user dispatch quota exhaustion, host-wide scheduler saturation, and thundering-herd patterns that align many cron schedules to the same minute. The narrative complements sibling gold cards without replacing them. UC-13.1.10 remains authoritative for search head cluster captain health, artifact replication, and member flapping. UC-13.1.11 remains the indexer cluster replication and bucket fixup story. UC-13.1.4 remains enterprise license violation analytics rather than per-user quota starvation. UC-13.1.3 remains universal forwarder connectivity and phone-home style telemetry rather than search-tier concurrency. UC-13.1.15 remains certificate and TLS trust drift for distributed management. This UC answers whether scheduled knowledge objects are being skipped because the platform cannot obtain a search slot, because quotas deny dispatch, or because operators intentionally disabled work during incidents.

Ground every deployment in scheduler.log semantics on index=_internal with sourcetype=scheduler. The status field distinguishes skipped versus success versus informational transitions. The reason field carries human-readable skip explanations that you must treat as structured telemetry rather than prose: phrases mentioning the maximum number of concurrent historical scheduled searches point to host-wide or role-aware concurrency caps. Quota-oriented language points to dispatch quota per user, max per-user quota, or authorize.conf derived ceilings. Disabled skips differ materially: a saved search marked disabled in savedsearches.conf or through the REST saved searches endpoint should not page like a capacity incident unless an unexpected disable indicates compromise or a failed deployment. Real-time scheduled work interacts with rtsearch quotas and can crowd out historical runners when misconfigured. savedsearch_id and app identify the knowledge object; user identifies the effective runner identity that dispatch uses for quota accounting. Compare user to owner when investigating accidental admin-role inflation that grants enormous srchJobsQuota.

limits.conf stanza [scheduler] is the configuration backbone for sustainable scheduled throughput. max_searches_per_cpu scales baseline historical scheduled concurrency against CPU inventory on the search head or search head cluster member. auto_summary_perc reserves scheduler budget for summarization and should not be starved without architecture review. base_max_searches and related keys interact with version-specific defaults; always reconcile effective settings with btool merge limits after upgrades. dispatch_quota_per_user and max_per_user_quota shape fairness when many service accounts own dense schedules. max_action_results and subsearch limits indirectly pressure scheduled searches that fan out huge result sets through dispatch.max_count or runaway subsearches. authorize.conf role stanzas map users to cumulative search quotas, disk quotas, and realtime search limits; a single mistaken role assignment can turn a human account into a noisy neighbor.

savedsearches.conf cron alignment drives thundering herds. A fleet that stamps fifty independent reports at zero minutes past the hour creates a predictable spike that collides with summary maintenance, scripted input bursts, and security correlation schedules. Randomized delay or staggered minute offsets are operational hygiene. dispatch.ttl, dispatch.earliest_time, and dispatch.latest_time influence how long searches occupy slots. schedule_window changes late-start tolerance; misunderstanding schedule windows can look like mysterious skips when searches intentionally defer. Real-time scheduled searches and continuous scheduling modes change how skipped reasons read versus purely cron-driven historical jobs.

Differentiate disabled versus quota versus concurrency_limit skips before you page executives. Disabled skips often correlate with deliberate change tickets. Quota skips often correlate with role changes or new premium application installs that add dozens of concurrent jobs. Concurrency_limit skips often correlate with CPU shrink events, virtualization oversubscription, or a bug-fix release that lowers max_searches_per_cpu. System overload reasons may combine disk wait, search process crashes, or bundle replication stalls; pair this UC with Monitoring Console workload and with UC-13.1.10 when SHC instability shifts effective capacity.

Search head cluster deployments duplicate scheduler.log per member while captain coordination decides which scheduled artifacts run where. Deployer-managed configurations should keep savedsearches.conf consistent across members, yet introspection still shows member-local active_hist_searches. When a member leaves service, remaining members absorb cron bursts; skipped counts may jump even though no configuration change occurred. Indexer tier does not emit scheduler skips for search head work; do not misroute indexer on-call when the signal is purely search_scheduler skipping on a search head member.

Risk briefing: legitimate maintenance includes mass-disabling searches during ingestion incidents; skipped volume then reflects good hygiene, not platform failure. License violations can trigger auto-tuning that reduces concurrency; correlate with UC-13.1.4 timelines before blaming application owners. Splunk Cloud resource pools can change effective quotas without a customer editing a local file; keep change records from the service provider alongside internal runbooks. DST transitions and clock skew can create duplicate ticks or missed ticks on some operating systems; macOS historical DST bugs are a known class of one-off scheduler anomalies that require time hygiene checks. Conf migration that removes a CSV-based inputlookup driver can make enrichment joins silently sparse without skipping the underlying scheduler events; validate lookup freshness when severities spike after migrations.

Field normalization expectations: coalesce ladders must absorb splunk_server versus host naming, savedsearch_id versus savedsearch_name in older builds, and missing app or user stand-in values introduced by malformed knowledge objects. Security reviewers should treat user and savedsearch_id as potentially sensitive in regulated estates; restrict dashboard drilldown roles. Legal reviewers should confirm _audit action=search retention meets investigation policy.

Collector posture: forward _internal and _introspection from every search head and SHC member to a security analytics index with tiered retention: hot fourteen days for operations, warm ninety days for trend analytics, and hold _audit per enterprise records policy. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance events in a sidecar index so correlations resist false escalation during approved windows.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes that multisearch fuses without pretending scheduler.log alone explains quota economics. Lane one is index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler status=skipped, capturing reason, savedsearch_id, app, user, and temporal fields that map to dispatch_time and run_time when present in your minor version. Lane two is the same sourcetype with status=success to compute skip_rate_pct denominators per fifteen-minute bucket. Lane three is index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with component=DispatchManager OR component=SearchScheduler, capturing throttling language that contextualizes skips when scheduler reasons are terse. Lane four is index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage with component=PerProcess and non-null search_props.app, approximating observed historical concurrency using active_hist_searches or active_searches depending on build. Lane five is index=_audit action=search for user-level audit volume that helps distinguish organic search storms from scheduled starvation.

props.conf for sourcetype=scheduler should preserve LINE_BREAKER semantics so multi-line scheduler exceptions remain intact. TRUNCATE should be high enough that long reason strings survive for analytics. TIME_PREFIX and TIME_FORMAT must honor the timestamp emitted by splunkd; drift here misaligns fifteen-minute bins and breaks streamstats burst detection. For splunk_resource_usage, confirm introspection engines are enabled on search heads per vendor guidance; disabling introspection to save disk removes the concurrency arm and blinds this UC.

Lookup maintenance: maintain splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv keyed on savedsearch_id with owner_user, owner_team, inv_app renamed from app inside the join to avoid field collisions, business_priority, runbook_url, cron_schedule, and expected_run_minutes. Refresh from version control whenever deployment pipelines promote saved searches. CMDB alignment: index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_search_head_inventory must expose search_head, shc_label, and env_tier with daily freshness. Stale CMDB produces routing gaps, not false skips, but on-call narratives degrade.

Monitoring Console workload dashboards validate that introspection-derived concurrency aligns with interactive experience during drills. Distributed Management Console patterns remain relevant on older estates; modern Monitoring Console views should show search head scheduler latency and concurrent searches. Pair with alerts for abrupt drops in success counts when skipped counts are flat to detect upstream ingestion loss of scheduler logs rather than silent health.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

```bash
splunk btool limits list scheduler --debug
splunk btool savedsearches list --debug | head
index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler status=skipped earliest=-1h | stats count by reason
```

Pre-save validation: scheduler skipped and success events must appear on active search heads within the same fifteen-minute windows you bin in production. Missing streams imply wrong forwarder routes, index filtering, or RBAC preventing analysts from reading _internal. _introspection must return PerProcess rows with search_props fields on at least one search head in each pool.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_5_scheduled_search_skip_concurrency with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate search_head plus savedsearch_id rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include owner_team, business_priority, and recommended_action tokens in pager narratives so operators open the correct runbook slice without rerunning ad hoc multisearch triage.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms so a silent single lane does not hide a genuine dispatch crisis. eventstats broadcasts splunkd dispatch pressure and introspection concurrency to skip and success rows keyed by search_head, user, and app before the final stats collapse. streamstats window=2 on fifteen-minute bins approximates a trailing thirty-minute skip burst for sustained criticality. eventstats median skip_rate_pct by search_head adds fleet-relative context for analysts. case maps combined skip rate, burst volume, quota-heavy reasons, and dispatch pressure into severity tiers. join enriches savedsearch metadata from splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv and CMDB search head inventory for routing.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.5 scheduled-search skip fuse: scheduler skipped and success, splunkd DispatchManager SearchScheduler, introspection PerProcess concurrency, audit search volume. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now status=skipped
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id, savedsearch_name, "", ""))))
      | eval skip_reason=coalesce(reason, message, "unknown")
      | eval lane="skip"
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS cnt_skip dc(skip_reason) AS distinct_skip_reasons values(skip_reason) AS skip_reason_mv mode(skip_reason) AS top_skip_reason BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now status=success
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id, savedsearch_name, "", ""))))
      | eval lane="ok"
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS cnt_ok BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval distinct_skip_reasons=0
      | eval top_skip_reason="n_a" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=DispatchManager OR component=SearchScheduler)
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval dispatch_pressure=if(match(lm,"quota|concurrent|throttle|skip|overload|saturation|maximum number of concurrent"),1,0)
      | stats max(dispatch_pressure) AS dispatch_pressure BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="splunkd"
      | eval user="*"
      | eval app="*"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now component=PerProcess
      | where isnotnull('search_props.app')
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.app', app, "", ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.user', user, "", ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | eval conc=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, active_searches, 0))
      | stats perc95(conc) AS concurrency_at_skip_p95 max(conc) AS peak_concurrency_observed BY _time search_head user app
      | eval lane="intro"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now action=search
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS audit_search_volume BY _time search_head user
      | eval lane="audit"
      | eval app="*"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="splunkd",dispatch_pressure,null()))) AS dispatch_pressure_sh BY _time search_head
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="intro",peak_concurrency_observed,null()))) AS peak_concurrency_user_app BY _time search_head user app
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="intro",concurrency_at_skip_p95,null()))) AS conc_p95_user_app BY _time search_head user app
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="audit",audit_search_volume,null()))) AS audit_search_volume_sh_user BY _time search_head user
| where lane IN ("skip","ok")
| stats sum(cnt_skip) AS skipped_count_15min sum(cnt_ok) AS success_count max(distinct_skip_reasons) AS distinct_skip_reasons values(skip_reason_mv) AS skip_reason_mv first(top_skip_reason) AS top_skip_reason max(dispatch_pressure_sh) AS dispatch_pressure max(peak_concurrency_user_app) AS peak_concurrency_observed max(conc_p95_user_app) AS concurrency_at_skip_p95 max(audit_search_volume_sh_user) AS audit_search_volume BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
| fillnull value=0 skipped_count_15min success_count distinct_skip_reasons dispatch_pressure
| eval denom=skipped_count_15min+success_count
| eval skip_rate_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*skipped_count_15min/denom,3), 0)
| sort 0 search_head user app savedsearch_id - _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=t sum(skipped_count_15min) AS skipped_sliding_30m BY search_head user app savedsearch_id
| eventstats median(skip_rate_pct) AS fleet_skip_rate_median BY search_head
| eval top_skip_reason=if(top_skip_reason=="n_a" OR isnull(top_skip_reason), coalesce(mvindex(skip_reason_mv,0),"unknown"), top_skip_reason)
| join type=left savedsearch_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv
     | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(savedsearch_id)))
     | rename app AS inv_app
     | fields savedsearch_id owner_user owner_team inv_app business_priority runbook_url cron_schedule expected_run_minutes ]
| eval user=coalesce(user, owner_user)
| eval app=coalesce(if(app=="*",null(),app), inv_app)
| join type=left search_head
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_search_head_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head, host, ""))))
      | dedup search_head
      | fields search_head shc_label env_tier ]
| eval severity=case(
    (skip_rate_pct>5 AND skipped_sliding_30m>0) OR skipped_count_15min>50 OR (like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%quota%") AND distinct_skip_reasons>=3), "critical",
    skip_rate_pct>2 OR skipped_count_15min>20 OR dispatch_pressure==1, "high",
    skip_rate_pct>0.5 OR skipped_count_15min>5, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%concurrent%"), "stagger_cron_raise_limits_scheduler_max_searches_per_cpu_review_savedsearch_alignment",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%quota%"), "reduce_role_quota_pressure_review_authorize_limits_and_dispatch_tuning",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND dispatch_pressure==1, "inspect_dispatch_manager_search_scheduler_splunkd_and_mc_workload",
    severity="medium", "tune_schedules_spread_top_of_hour_bursts_validate_new_app_search_surge",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_validate_inventory_lookup_freshness")
| table _time search_head user app savedsearch_id owner_team business_priority skipped_count_15min skip_rate_pct top_skip_reason peak_concurrency_observed severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_5_scheduled_search_skip_concurrency_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_5_scheduled_search_skip_concurrency | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal service level objectives, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on search_head, user, app, and savedsearch_id, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap subsearch fan-out on tiny search heads.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the search head under study. Open scheduler.log locally and compare raw skipped lines to Splunk rows for the same wall clock minute. Verify reason strings match and that savedsearch_id parsing aligns with the knowledge object name in the REST saved searches listing.

Use Monitoring Console search workload and scheduler views as a second opinion: peaks should align between console visuals and platform_uc_13_1_5 rows within one fifteen-minute bin. When they diverge, verify that the monitoring search head can read all pool members and that SSL or firewall changes did not partition a site.

Run btool merge limits for [scheduler] and reconcile effective max_searches_per_cpu with hardware counts and virtualization ratios. Compare authorize.conf role quotas for the busiest service accounts surfaced in the alert. Inspect savedsearches.conf cron_schedule for top-of-hour alignment among the noisiest objects.

Deliberate maintenance test: disable a non-production saved search under change control, confirm skipped reasons show disabled language, and confirm severity stays low when inventory marks the object as intentionally dormant. Restore the search and confirm success counts return.

Negative test: on a calm search head after a quiet weekend, confirm critical and high severities stay empty for four consecutive runs when thresholds are temporarily mirrored in a clone with tightened guardrails.

Job Inspector: confirm scanned event counts remain proportional to _internal volume; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded wildcards in macros.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Scheduled report misaligned to top-of-hour stampede: shift cron minutes using staggered offsets, introduce schedule_window where appropriate, and validate executive dashboards still meet delivery contracts.

Case 2 — Runaway saved search bypass via dispatch.max_count: inspect result sets, tighten time bounds, replace naive joins with summaries, and teach authors about subsearch limits.

Case 3 — Ad-hoc user accidentally granted admin role inflating quota: revert role mapping in authorize.conf, enforce least privilege in identity provider groups, and replay _audit for privilege additions.

Case 4 — Search head cluster member outage shifting load: confirm UC-13.1.10 health, rebalance scheduled work if captain policies allow, and restore the member before forcing dense cron on survivors.

Case 5 — Enterprise Security correlation searches added without capacity budget: negotiate schedule offsets with security operations, use workload management rules, and add app-specific inventory rows with business_priority for fair routing.

Case 6 — New app installed with fifty hourly searches: require onboarding review, spread minutes, and consider dedicated search head pools for bursty premium workloads.

Case 7 — ITSI KPI base searches skipped due to quota: align service owner expectations, tune KPI search schedules, and coordinate with ITSI admins on backfill windows.

Case 8 — License auto-tune halving concurrency under violation: correlate with license master messaging, remediate ingestion compliance, and document temporary capacity loss with executive stakeholders.

Case 9 — Splunk Cloud resource pool change lowering effective quotas: open provider ticket, compare before-and-after workload screenshots, and reschedule heavy jobs after confirmation.

Case 10 — Conf migration removing CSV inputlookup driver: restore lookup generation pipeline, validate splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv row counts, and backfill missing metadata before re-enabling pages.

Case 11 — macOS DST clock shift creating duplicated scheduler tick: validate NTP strata, compare _internal time skew alerts, and pause severity during the documented one-hour anomaly window after vendor guidance.

Case 12 — Bug-fix release lowered max_searches_per_cpu: read release notes, compare btool effective limits before and after, restore prior caps only under architecture sign-off, or add search head capacity rather than fighting supported defaults.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists scheduler skipped, scheduler success, splunkd dispatch, introspection PerProcess, and _audit arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; closing table includes owner_team and business_priority.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.1.5 scheduled-search skip fuse: scheduler skipped and success, splunkd DispatchManager SearchScheduler, introspection PerProcess concurrency, audit search volume. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now status=skipped
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id, savedsearch_name, "", ""))))
      | eval skip_reason=coalesce(reason, message, "unknown")
      | eval lane="skip"
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS cnt_skip dc(skip_reason) AS distinct_skip_reasons values(skip_reason) AS skip_reason_mv mode(skip_reason) AS top_skip_reason BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now status=success
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app, "", ""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id, savedsearch_name, "", ""))))
      | eval lane="ok"
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS cnt_ok BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval distinct_skip_reasons=0
      | eval top_skip_reason="n_a" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=DispatchManager OR component=SearchScheduler)
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | eval lm=lower(_raw)
      | eval dispatch_pressure=if(match(lm,"quota|concurrent|throttle|skip|overload|saturation|maximum number of concurrent"),1,0)
      | stats max(dispatch_pressure) AS dispatch_pressure BY _time search_head
      | eval lane="splunkd"
      | eval user="*"
      | eval app="*"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now component=PerProcess
      | where isnotnull('search_props.app')
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval app=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.app', app, "", ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('search_props.user', user, "", ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | eval conc=tonumber(coalesce(active_hist_searches, active_searches, 0))
      | stats perc95(conc) AS concurrency_at_skip_p95 max(conc) AS peak_concurrency_observed BY _time search_head user app
      | eval lane="intro"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now action=search
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval user=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(user, "", ""))))
      | bin span=15m _time
      | stats count AS audit_search_volume BY _time search_head user
      | eval lane="audit"
      | eval app="*"
      | eval savedsearch_id="*"
      | eval cnt_skip=0
      | eval cnt_ok=0 ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="splunkd",dispatch_pressure,null()))) AS dispatch_pressure_sh BY _time search_head
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="intro",peak_concurrency_observed,null()))) AS peak_concurrency_user_app BY _time search_head user app
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="intro",concurrency_at_skip_p95,null()))) AS conc_p95_user_app BY _time search_head user app
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="audit",audit_search_volume,null()))) AS audit_search_volume_sh_user BY _time search_head user
| where lane IN ("skip","ok")
| stats sum(cnt_skip) AS skipped_count_15min sum(cnt_ok) AS success_count max(distinct_skip_reasons) AS distinct_skip_reasons values(skip_reason_mv) AS skip_reason_mv first(top_skip_reason) AS top_skip_reason max(dispatch_pressure_sh) AS dispatch_pressure max(peak_concurrency_user_app) AS peak_concurrency_observed max(conc_p95_user_app) AS concurrency_at_skip_p95 max(audit_search_volume_sh_user) AS audit_search_volume BY _time search_head user app savedsearch_id
| fillnull value=0 skipped_count_15min success_count distinct_skip_reasons dispatch_pressure
| eval denom=skipped_count_15min+success_count
| eval skip_rate_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*skipped_count_15min/denom,3), 0)
| sort 0 search_head user app savedsearch_id - _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=t sum(skipped_count_15min) AS skipped_sliding_30m BY search_head user app savedsearch_id
| eventstats median(skip_rate_pct) AS fleet_skip_rate_median BY search_head
| eval top_skip_reason=if(top_skip_reason=="n_a" OR isnull(top_skip_reason), coalesce(mvindex(skip_reason_mv,0),"unknown"), top_skip_reason)
| join type=left savedsearch_id
    [| inputlookup splunk_savedsearch_inventory.csv
     | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(savedsearch_id)))
     | rename app AS inv_app
     | fields savedsearch_id owner_user owner_team inv_app business_priority runbook_url cron_schedule expected_run_minutes ]
| eval user=coalesce(user, owner_user)
| eval app=coalesce(if(app=="*",null(),app), inv_app)
| join type=left search_head
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=splunk_search_head_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval search_head=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(search_head, host, ""))))
      | dedup search_head
      | fields search_head shc_label env_tier ]
| eval severity=case(
    (skip_rate_pct>5 AND skipped_sliding_30m>0) OR skipped_count_15min>50 OR (like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%quota%") AND distinct_skip_reasons>=3), "critical",
    skip_rate_pct>2 OR skipped_count_15min>20 OR dispatch_pressure==1, "high",
    skip_rate_pct>0.5 OR skipped_count_15min>5, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%concurrent%"), "stagger_cron_raise_limits_scheduler_max_searches_per_cpu_review_savedsearch_alignment",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND like(lower(top_skip_reason),"%quota%"), "reduce_role_quota_pressure_review_authorize_limits_and_dispatch_tuning",
    severity IN ("critical","high") AND dispatch_pressure==1, "inspect_dispatch_manager_search_scheduler_splunkd_and_mc_workload",
    severity="medium", "tune_schedules_spread_top_of_hour_bursts_validate_new_app_search_surge",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_validate_inventory_lookup_freshness")
| table _time search_head user app savedsearch_id owner_team business_priority skipped_count_15min skip_rate_pct top_skip_reason peak_concurrency_observed severity recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS search_head
| join type=left max=0 search_head [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Inventory.os) AS os FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest
| rename Inventory.dest AS search_head ]
| where cpu_pct>85 OR mem_used>90
| table search_head cpu_pct mem_used os
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for search head hosts when correlating CPU and memory pressure with skip bursts; accelerate Inventory summaries for fleet metadata joins on Inventory.dest matching search_head naming; retain at least thirty days for quarterly operational attestations.
visualization: Fifteen-minute timechart of skipped_count_15min and success denominators by search_head; severity-colored table with drilldowns to raw scheduler and splunkd lines; overlay peak_concurrency_observed from introspection; workload tiles from Monitoring Console deep links.

references.title: Splunk Docs — limits.conf dispatch settings anchor
Splunk Docs — Configure the search scheduler

knownFalsePositives: Planned mass-disable of scheduled searches during a sev-1 ingestion incident legitimately spikes skipped counts with disabled reasons; require change-ticket correlation before paging application owners. Deliberate canary searches that intentionally skip to prove alerting paths can look like quota failure until inventory marks them as test objects. A search head peer outage concentrates cron on survivors; pair skip bursts with UC-13.1.10 timelines rather than assuming misconfiguration. Big quarterly roll-up jobs that run once per quarter create a single loud bin; compare against finance-approved calendars before opening capacity projects. Scheduled patch windows that restart many members in sequence can depress success denominators briefly; suppress using maintenance metadata stamps. Indexer-side slowdowns can indirectly starve search heads when bundle replication or distributed search fan-out stalls; cross-check distributed search health before blaming scheduler limits alone. Stale inventory lookups make enrichment sparse while raw skips remain real; route low-confidence rows to hygiene dashboards instead of executive pages. Executive read-only walk-throughs that trigger many interactive searches can elevate _audit volume without implying scheduled quota exhaustion; distinguish action=search audit noise from scheduler skip rates.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.10 search head cluster captain and artifact replication monitoring, UC-13.1.11 indexer replication health, UC-13.1.4 enterprise license violation economics in place of per-user dispatch quotas, UC-13.1.3 forwarder connectivity programs, UC-13.1.15 certificate trust analytics, or interactive-only search slow-down triage without scheduled skip evidence; does not automatically mutate limits.conf; produces routing guidance for human-approved changes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab search head, induce skipped scheduler events with concurrency language while introspection shows elevated active_hist_searches, confirm platform_uc_13_1_5 returns critical or high within two fifteen-minute windows with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After staggering cron minutes and restoring normal quotas, with Monitoring Console workload green and scheduler success denominators stable, confirm severities return to low or medium for tested saved searches and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

evidence: Saved search platform_uc_13_1_5_scheduled_search_skip_concurrency with fifteen minute schedule; Monitoring Console workload drilldowns; alert routing to platform search on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.
hardware: Applies to Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud customer-managed search heads, search head clusters with deployer-managed bundles, and standalone search heads on Linux or container hosts when _internal, _introspection, and _audit are forwarded to the analytics tier.
